zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan Bourchier於1440年出生在英國艾可哈爾斯亞德時，她的父親Sir John Bourchier, 1st Baron Berners25歲，母親Margery Berners22歲。她於1462年與Sir Henry Neville在英國艾可哈爾斯亞德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1470年10月7日在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃里克享年30歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Joan Bourchier于1440年出生在英国艾可哈爾斯亞德时，她的父亲Sir John Bourchier, 1st Baron Berners25岁，母亲Margery Berners22岁。她于1462年与Sir Henry Neville在英国艾可哈爾斯亞德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1470年10月7日在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾沃里克享年30岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾沃里克。

nameMeanings.name: Bourchier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German   Buser  .
English (Kent): variant of   Bowser   1.

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: North German (also Börger) and Dutch: variant of Burger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from Yiddish borger ‘borrower’ or ‘lender’.
Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name for someone from a place called Borg (see   Borg  ), or a topographic name for someone living by a fortification or near a hill resembling a fortification.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan Bourchier was born in 1440, in Halstead, Essex, England, her father, Sir John Bourchier, 1st Baron Berners, was 25 and her mother, Margery Berners, was 22. She married Sir Henry Neville in 1462, in Halstead, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 7 October 1470, in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 30, and was buried in Warwick, Warwickshire, England.

Elle a épousé Sir Henry Neville en 1462, à Halstead, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 7 octobre 1470, à Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterrée à Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Bourchier(이)가 1440년 영국 에세스 할스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Bourchier, 1st Baron Berners(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Berners(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1462년 영국 에세스 할스테아드에서 Sir Henry Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1470년 10월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 워윅에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Joan Bourchier родилась в 1440 г. в местности холстид, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Bourchier, 1st Baron Berners было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Berners, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Henry Neville в 1462 г. в местности холстид, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 7 октября 1470 г. в местности варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 30 лет и была похоронена в местности варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия.
